nameMeanings.name: Wilmot
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Will(i)mot(t), a hypocoristic form of Willelm (see William ). Compare the Old French personal name Guillemot. This surname has been established in Ireland (Kerry) since 1614.
Walloon: from a pet form of the personal name   William  . Compare   Wilmont  .
History: Benjamin Wilmot and his wife, with their 6-year-old son William, emigrated from England to New Haven, CT, in or before 1640.

variants.name: Wilmoth
meanings: English: variant of   Wilmot  .

variants.name: Willmott
meanings: English: variant of   Wilmot  .

variants.name: Wilmont
meanings: Altered form of Walloon or English Wilmot . The surname Wilmont is also found in Belgium (Wallonia) and northern France, but it is very rare there.
In some cases probably also Dutch: altered form of Walloon Wilmot or of its very rare French cognate Willemont.

variants.name: Wilmarth
meanings: English: variant of   Wilmot  .
History: Thomas Wilmarth (1620–90) came to North America in 1639. He was one ofthe proprietors of Rehoboth, MA, in 1643.

variants.name: Wilmeth
meanings: English: variant of   Wilmot  .

variants.name: William
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English personal name William, Willam, Willem, an Old Picard and Norman French form of ancient Germanic Willihelm, composed of the elements wil- ‘will, desire’ + helm- ‘helmet, protection’. This Norman personal name became widely used in England, Scotland and Wales after the Conquest, mainly no doubt in honor of the Conqueror himself. By the 13th century it had already become the most popular personal name in England. A Parisian form of the name Guillaume was also sometimes used (see Gillam ), which in medieval Wales was adopted as Gwilym (see Gwilliam ).
Americanized form (translation into English) of French   Guillaume  .

variants.name: Wilmott
meanings: English: variant of   Wilmot  .

nameMeanings.name: Edward
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + weard ‘guard’. This has been one of the most successful of all Old English names, in frequent use from before the Conquest to the present day, and even being exported into other European languages. It was the name of three Anglo-Saxon kings and has been borne by eight kings of England since the Norman Conquest. It is also the name of the youngest son of Queen Elizabeth II . The most influential early bearer was King Edward the Confessor ( ?1002–66 ; ruled 1042–66 ). In a troubled period of English history, he contrived to rule fairly and (for a time at any rate) firmly. But in the latter part of his reign he paid more attention to his religion than to his kingdom. He died childless, and his death sparked off conflicting claims to his throne, which were resolved by the victory of William the Conqueror at the Battle of Hastings. His memory was honoured by Normans and English alike, for his fairness and his piety. Edward's mother was Norman; he had spent part of his youth in Normandy; and William claimed to have been nominated by Edward as his successor. Edward was canonized in the 12th century, and came to be venerated throughout Europe as a model of a Christian king.

variants.name: Edoardo
meanings: From English. See Edward .

historicEvents.title: Book of Common Prayer
historicEvents.description: Book of Common Prayer, a product of the English Reformation, was published in 1549 for assistance in the administration of the sacraments and other rites and ceremonies of the church according to the use of the Church of England. The book outlined morning, evening, and communion prayers and orders for baptism and marriage, thus making England a truly Protestant state.

historicEvents.title: Act of Uniformity
historicEvents.description: The Act of Uniformity was passed by the Parliament of England and required all people to go to church once a week. The consequence of not attending church was a fine of 12 pence, which was a considerable amount for a poor person.

historicEvents.title: State Lottery
historicEvents.description: A State Lottery was recorded in 1569. The tickets were sold at St. Paul's Cathedral in London.

historicEvents.title: The First English Colony is Established in America
historicEvents.description: England established its first colony in North America in 1585 as a way to generate additional wealth. The colony was named Virginia, after the virgin Queen Elizabeth I.

historicEvents.title: Elizabeth I Signs Death Warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots
historicEvents.description: Elizabeth I signed the death warrant for Mary, Queen of Scots, on February 1, 1587, as she was found guilty of plotting to assasinate Elizabeth. Mary, Queen of Scots, was beheaded at Fotheringhay Castle in England on February 8, 1587.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Elizabeth I.org - Mary Queen of Scots

historicEvents.title: The Honourable East India Company
historicEvents.description: The Honourable East India Company, a British joint-stock company, was established in 1600 for trade in the Indian Ocean region. At its height, the British East India Company had a private army which was twice the size of the British Army, ruled large sections of India, and revenues in the millions.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Historic UK - The East India Company

en.lifeSummary: When Edward Wilmot was born in 1542, in South Oxfordshire, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, George Willmote or Wilmarth, was 53 and his mother, Christiana Bustard, was 42. He married Elizabeth Stafford after 1570, in Bradfield, Berkshire, England. They were the parents of at least 2 sons. He died in 1602, in Witney, Oxfordshire, England, at the age of 60.

Il a épousé Elizabeth Stafford après 1570, à Bradfield, Berkshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 2 fils. Il est décédé en 1602, à Witney, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 60 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Edward Wilmot(이)가 1542년 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 소우스 스퍼드실르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Willmote or Wilmarth(은)는 53세였고, 그의 어머니 Christiana Bustard(은)는 42세였다. 그는 1570년 이후 영국 버크실르 브래드필드에서 Elizabeth Stafford(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1602년 영국 스퍼드실르 윗니에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

Он женился на женщине по имени Elizabeth Stafford после 1570 г. в местности брадфиелд, биркшир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 2 сына.
